perturbation.original_text: A. Subcontractor shall be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Subcontractor shall not be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 6.A from 'shall be permitted' to 'shall not be permitted' contradicts the original intention of allowing subcontracting without prior consent. This creates an in-text contradiction as it now implies that subcontracting is prohibited without explicit permission, undermining the subcontractor's autonomy and introducing uncertainty regarding the circumstances under which subcontracting is allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Subcontractor shall not be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall be permitted' to 'shall not be permitted' directly contradicts the initial permission for subcontracting. The original text grants permission for subcontracting without prior consent, while the changed text explicitly prohibits it without EDGE's approval. The contradicted text states that 'Subcontractor shall not be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE', which conflicts with the original clause that allows subcontracting without prior consent. This directly contradicts the subcontractor's ability to freely subcontract, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on conflicting obligations.
